sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, created_at TIMESTAMP(3) WITH TIME ZONE, description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, created_at, description) VALUES (1, timestamp(3) with time zone '2020-09-28 18:40:05.123+02:00', 'Test Entry 1');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, created_at, description) VALUES (2, timestamp(3) with time zone '2021-01-01 00:00:00.000+00:00', NULL);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, created_at, description) VALUES (3, timestamp(3) with time zone current_timestamp() + interval '1 day', 'Test Entry 2');
SELECT id, current_timestamp() - created_at AS time_difference FROM test_table WHERE created_at IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP() AS now_timestamp, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE created_at < CURRENT_TIMESTAMP()) AS count_before_now FROM test_table;
SELECT id, description, timestamp(3) WITH TIME ZONE '2020-09-28 18:40:05.123+02:00' + interval '1 hour' AS new_timestamp FROM test_table WHERE created_at = (SELECT MAX(created_at) FROM test_table);
CREATE TABLE another_table (data TIMESTAMP(3) WITH TIME ZONE, tag VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO another_table (data, tag) VALUES (timestamp(3) with time zone '2020-09-28 12:00:00.000+02:00', 'Initial Entry');
SELECT a.id, a.created_at, COALESCE(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (current_timestamp() - a.created_at)), 0) AS age_seconds FROM test_table a JOIN another_table b ON a.created_at = b.data;
WITH time_summary AS (SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM created_at) AS year, COUNT(*) AS entry_count FROM test_table GROUP BY year HAVING COUNT(*) > 1) SELECT * FROM time_summary;
ALTER TABLE test_table ADD COLUMN updated_at TIMESTAMP(3) WITH TIME ZONE;
UPDATE test_table SET updated_at = timestamp(3) with time zone current_timestamp() WHERE id = 1;
SELECT id, CASE WHEN description IS NULL THEN 'No Description' ELSE description END AS adjusted_description FROM test_table ORDER BY adjusted_description;
